name: Rice Flour & Yogurt Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, sift together the rice flour, cornstarch, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
2. In another bowl, whisk together the egg, vegetable oil, and yogurt.
3. Gradually add the liquid ingredients to the dry ingredients, stirring until just combined.
4. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
5. Spoon batter by tablespoonfuls onto the pan.
6. Cook pancakes for 2 minutes or less on each side, until golden brown.
7. Stack on warm plates. Serve with butter and preserves, or honey.
Makes 26 pancakes, 2-3/4 inches in diameter.
If making fewer than six pancakes, reserve the remaining dry and liquid ingredients separately and combine just before cooking. The flour mixture can be stored refrigerated for up to weeks, while the liquid mixture lasts for up to 3 days.
Per 2 Pancakes: 75 calories, 2 g protein, 11 g carbohydrate, 3 g fat (1 g saturated), 18 mg cholesterol, 62 mg sodium, 0 g fiber.
